Semester_Project

This is the Semester Project for the course AD688 - Cloud Analytics for Business (Spring 2026).

Topic: Salary & Compensation Disparity across Disciplines

Introduction

This report analyzes salary disparities across disciplines using Lightcast job postings data. The goal is to understand how salaries vary between AI and non-AI careers, across regions, and by job type (remote vs in-office). The study also explores skill demand and provides career recommendations.

Market Trends

AI vs Non-AI Salaries

AI-related roles show significantly higher salaries compared to non-AI roles. This is driven by high demand and limited supply of specialized talent.

Regional Differences

States such as California, Washington, and New York offer the highest salaries, particularly in technology roles.

Remote vs In-Office

Remote jobs tend to offer competitive or higher salaries, especially in AI-related fields.

Industry Trends

Industries such as Information, Finance, and Professional Services show the highest average salaries.

Skill Analysis

The most in-demand skills include:

  • Python
  • SQL
  • Machine Learning
  • Cloud Computing (AWS, Azure)

There is a clear skill gap in advanced AI capabilities, indicating strong opportunities for skilled professionals.

Career Strategy Recommendations

Short-Term

  • Learn Python, SQL, and data analysis tools

Mid-Term

  • Gain experience in machine learning and cloud platforms

Long-Term

  • Specialize in AI domains such as NLP or Deep Learning

Conclusion

Salary disparities highlight the growing importance of AI-related skills in the labor market. Professionals who invest in high-demand technical skills can access significantly higher-paying opportunities and long-term career growth.
